characters.name: Boo Boo
characters.description: Boo-Boo Whiteis a fictional character appearing in theRuby Gloomfranchise. He is depicted asGloom Manor's resident ghost whose job is to scare his roommates. Much tohis mentors'chagrin, however, Boo-Boo enjoys pranking more than scaring. "I love scaring everyone and anyone! My favorite thing to do is coming up with new pranks and ways to catch my friends off guard (the look on Scaredy Bat's face is priceless). Because, being a ghost,  that's what I do! I think I'm pretty good at it, too; but that's because I really apply myself. And for the record, I'm not cute! Grrr..." "Boo-Boo is the annoying little rascal of a ghost who is constantly trying to frighten everyone in the house. Unfortunately he is so darn cute that he is not to successful at scaring anyone. He is like a pesky little brother who likes to push everyone's buttons and is constantly looking for an opportunity to pull a prank." Despite his job as a scarer, Boo-Boo serves as more of a prank-happy nuisance than a menace. He seems to be good friends withScaredy Bat, despite Scaredy being his main victim.
characters.personality_section: Despite his job as a scarer, Boo-Boo serves as more of a prank-happy nuisance than a menace. He seems to be good friends withScaredy Bat, despite Scaredy being his main victim.

characters.name: Doom Kitty
characters.description: Doom Kittyis the deuteragonist of theRuby Gloomfranchise, serving asthe title-character's pet cat. Doom has a complete free run of the mansion and is therefore usually the only one with total knowledge of what is going on in any given area of the house. This keeps her one step ahead of Ruby and the others most of the time, because whenever something strange happens in the mansion, Doom will usually have all the facts already while everyone else is still trying to figure things out. Since Doom can't talk, her only hope of communicating with the others is through charades, with violin sounds accompanying each of her motions. Ruby is the only one who routinely understands what Doom is trying to say. The others struggle to decipher her body language. Doom Kitty is a black cat with contrasting white eyelashes and a white nose, and a long, skinny tail she can bend in many shapes. She wears a red ribbon around her neck along with a little gold key with a heart-shaped hole. Doom is playful, clever, snarky, and helpful. She also loves Ruby dearly.
characters.personality_section: Doom is playful, clever, snarky, and helpful. She also loves Ruby dearly.

characters.name: Dust Bunnies
characters.description: Misery's dust bunniesare little dust creatures residing inGloom Manor.Miseryadopts them as her pets in "Pet Poepulation", in spite of her allergies. AtIris' suggestion, the banshee wears a surgical mask whenever she's with the dust bunnies to keep herself from sneezing. Aside from their inclusion in the aforementioned episode, the dust bunnies also make brief appearances throughout the rest of the series, often hopping out from underMiseryandRuby's beds or hanging out with Misery.

characters.name: Dust Bunnies

characters.name: Edgar and Allan
characters.description: Edgar, Allan, and Poe standing outside. Edgar and Allan, likePoe, are also dapper uptown ravens. They are both Poe's brothers. Edgar and Allan are hardly seen and when they are, they never speak. However, they seem to spend most of their time in the series acting as Poe's bodyguards, helpers and servants. Edgar wears a lowered top hat and a fancy hunter green coat. Allan dons the long top hat, dark eggplant purple coat, and a pair of eyeglasses.

characters.name: Edgar and Allan

characters.name: Frank and Len
characters.description: Frank and Lenare fictional characters appearing in theRuby Gloomfranchise. Rockers by nature, these conjoined twins live inGloom Manor's garage. They have their own garage band, known asR.I.P.. Frank and Len's strength lies in performing music. Unfortunately, this seems to be their only strength, since they can hardly focus on something if it doesn't have a beat. Frank is actually the more intelligent of the two, often getting annoyed at Len's lack of understanding. Len is the more sentimental one, who seems to have a thing for small animals and things that don't exactly matter in anyone else's book. Even though they seem upbeat for their music, the two of them are almost never on the same page, even though they're both in the same body. Despite this fact, Frank and Len are close. For example, in the episode "Quadro-Gloomia", when the brothers needed a way to become unconscious, they were unable to hit each other over the head with planks. Another example of this is in the episode "Iris Springs Eternal". Ruby, Frank and Len were sliding down a mud slide when Frank said, "Len, if we don't get out of this I just want you to know that you've been the best brother a brother could have for a brother." Also, as of the episode "Quadro-Gloomia", Frank and Len appear to be the only housemates (other thanDoom Kitty) who know aboutRuby's crush onSkull Boy. Frank: "Hey Len have you seen our guitar pick?" Len: "No Frank it's your turn to pick." Frank and Len are goofy, passionate about music, and dimwitted (especially Len).
characters.personality_section: Frank and Len are goofy, passionate about music, and dimwitted (especially Len).

characters.name: Gunther and Uta
characters.description: Gunther and Utaare German twins who stay atGloom Manoron vacation. The sauerkraut and sausage-loving, stereotypical German kids mistake Ruby and her friends for having ill intentions. They try their best to avoid and outwit the residents until the next trolley comes. Upon its arrival, the brother and sister frantically run to the trolley, with Uta's clog falling off her foot along the way. Iris tosses it to her and accidentally breaks the trolley. Desperate to get home because of their misunderstanding, Gunther and Uta get off and simply run. After this episode, they make a cameo in "Last Train to Gloomsville".

characters.name: Iris
characters.description: Irisis a fictional character appearing in theRuby Gloomfranchise. She is depicted as a hyperactive cyclops girl who loves to have fun and go on adventures. She is the owner ofSquig, a giant flying worm, andVenus, a venus flytrap. "There is just never enough adventures! No mountain too high... No creature too odd.. No valley too low! I love exploring and seeing new things! But I try to take Ruby's advice, and find fun in smaller things too.. Like my person eating plant Venus! People think I could slow down a bit and you know smell the flowers or you know 'read the instructions'. But that takes time and really - how much can I miss?" Iris has one purple eye. She is usually seen wearing a red bow in her black hair, a black dress with balloon sleeves, buttons, and a rounded flat collar with red accents, red and gray stockings, and black Mary Jane shoes with white accents. Iris is energetic, fun-loving, and thrill seeking. In the words ofRuby, enough is never enough for Iris. This, coupled with her carefree nature, causes her to be impulsive.
characters.personality_section: Iris is energetic, fun-loving, and thrill seeking. In the words ofRuby, enough is never enough for Iris. This, coupled with her carefree nature, causes her to be impulsive.

characters.name: Malady and Malaise
characters.description: Malady and MalaiseareMisery's cousins, who, when both are together, cause a localized ice age. Despite their faults, Misery loves her cousins. They first appeared in "Misery Loves Company". Later, they're seen in "I'll Be Home for Misery" and make silent cameos in "Last Train to Gloomsville". Malady is the one character that is more disease-prone than her cousin, Misery. Her arm is shown to be in a sling, and when asked about this, she'll ramble on and on about it for hours. Spending time with her alone will cause a tremendous headache, as experienced byPoe. Malaise suffers from narcolepsy and can often be seen lying on a couch or bed or even asleep on the floor randomly. She is accompanied by many creatures like a snake, spiders, an octopus, snails and butterflies which usually attach themselves to the full-body veil she wears. The pupils in her black eyes are red instead of white like Misery's. Spending time with her alone will cause one to lose steam, as experienced byIris.

characters.name: Millie
characters.description: Millieis the Tooth Fairy from the episode "Tooth or Dare". In the episode, she is tired of being a Tooth Fairy and quits, and then she feels down about not knowing what to do with the rest of her life. Millie loves math. Therefore, at the end of the episode, she gets a job as an accountant for the Tooth Fairy business at Fairy Headquarters. Millie is good friends withScaredy Batduring her stay atGloom Manor.

characters.name: Millie
characters.description: Millieis the Tooth Fairy from the episode "Tooth or Dare". In the episode, she is tired of being a Tooth Fairy and quits, and then she feels down about not knowing what to do with the rest of her life. Millie loves math. Therefore, at the end of the episode, she gets a job as an accountant for the Tooth Fairy business at Fairy Headquarters. Millie is good friends withScaredy Batduring her stay atGloom Manor.

characters.name: Misery
characters.description: Miseryis one of the tritagonists appearing in theRuby Gloomfranchise. She is depicted as a young banshee girl with long black hair, a constant flow of tears on her face, and a blue veil that she drags around the ground. She serves as the franchise's comic relief. "My Great, Great, Great, Great Grandmother's cousin on my father's side used to insist that our family had really bad luck. But I don't think that whole Ice Age thing was our fault. Besides, glaciers are... Pretty. Sure, I sleep on a bed of nails... But they say acupuncture is making a come back." Misery has big, sad, dark eyes with a constant flow of tears, and very long black hair with a blue veil that she drags around the ground. Her veil is held in place by two sunflower clips. She wears a long purple dress with long fringed sleeves, and a green brooch with a spider on it pinned to the frilly neck. She also wears a pair of black fingerless gloves. Misery is shy, very kind, clumsy, naive, loyal, and gloomy, though she sometimes has moments of pure happiness. She is usually melancholy, but the bad things that happen to her don't make her bitter, or even truly depressed. Every time misfortune strikes, she brushes it off and goes on with her day. She is actually sometimes shown to look on the bright side like Ruby, though Misery is obviously an off-beat optimist. From constantly being struck by lightning to catching various diseases to being chased by tornadoes, Misery is a walking disaster, owing to clumsiness and a curse of bad luck that runs in her family. At least she is skilled in being prepared for disasters and misfortunes. However, Friday the 13th inverts Misery's family's curse. Misery is also knowledgeable about great disasters and misfortunes in history. Despite her bad luck, Misery has actually been revealed to be an incredibly gifted singer. However, this talent only shines through whenever she's asleep. Otherwise, her singing just comes out as banshee-like shrieks. This madeFrank and Lenrecruit her as lead singer for their rock band,R.I.P., during a major concert event. Their performance was a huge success. The music video for Misery's lullaby, "Train Wreck", was shown in the opening sketch of the episode, "Skull in the Family".

characters.name: Misery's family
characters.description: Green (Morose) Miseryhas a lot of cousins and ancestors who strongly resemble her. In episode 37, "I'll Be Home for Misery" introduces Misery's relatives throughout the ages for their "once in a millennium" family reunion. The reunion can only last a short amount of time because of all the disasters that follow the family. In addition to these named family members, four others are shown. A relative that resembles Misery in a French Peasant Maid's outfit, another relative in a Medieval Princess gown and pointy hat, one dressed as a 1970s soul sister with a red dress and a white steak of hair like Frankenstein's bride, one with short hair, a stitched mouth, and a brown hat and coat, and one with white hair in braided pigtails curved up like bullhorns with a spider web in the middle. All of Misery's male relatives died in a quicksand except one, who happens to be her father.

characters.name: Morose
characters.description: Moroseis Misery's cousin who appeared in "I'll Be Home For Misery". Truly goth, Morose has dyed green/black hair and green and black striped stockings. Very thick streaks of eye shadow run down her face. She shows talent in singing and -unlike Misery- can sing while awake and performs a song with Frank and Len while playing keyboards. The boys love it until their house falls in around her. One of Morose's only happy moments all episode is here when she said "cool... another verse?".

characters.name: Mr. Buns
characters.description: Mr. Bunsis a stuffed rabbit thatRubycreated from one of her leftover socks. He is more than a toy to Ruby and her friends: he is a "special, one-of-a-kind friend". Only Misery thinks of him as a stuffed sock. After the others' disapproving shock, she refers to Mr. Buns as a "stuffed one-of-a-kind sock". The friends incorporate the floppy, yet enigmatic Mr. Buns in almost all the action in the mansion. He also has a strange tendency to end up anywhere and everywhere as if he were alive. It is possible that Mr. Buns could be possessed. "Mr Buns is the one of a kind sock puppet that I (Ruby) created. He also happens to be a good friend. He doesn't talk of move around too much. But, he does seem to some how go from one part of the house to another quite unexpectedly; it's almost like he has a life of his own! That cannot be possible... can it?" "Mr. Buns is the one of a kind sock puppet created by Ruby. He also happens to be a good friend. Mr. Buns does not speak or animate. However, he does seem to move around from one part of the house to another and appear quite unexpectedly; it's almost like he has a life of his own. That cannot be possible... can it?"

characters.name: Mr. Mummbles
characters.description: Mr. MummblesisRuby's elderly next-door neighbor. Mr. Mummbles lives in a glass house with his pet, a frog named Socrates. Mr. Mummbles received his name fromPoein the episode "Poe-Ranoia", where Poe thought that he was a murderer, and since then the name has stuck. After a revelation that Mr. Mummbles designs and creates amusement park rides, he can be seen in later episodes testing these new rides. His rides are designed to make people happy, sometimes having features that tickle the passengers. Mr. Mummbles also follows the rule "Safety First", however his rides seem to bend in the opposite direction to being safe, which includes massive spirals, gaps and fire on the tracks. He is voiced by an uncreditedDerek McGrath.

characters.name: Mr. White and Mr. White
characters.description: Mr. White and Mr. Whiteare the married ghostly mentors of youngBoo Boo. Distinguished by their glasses and high rank within "The Family", the husbands typically act in a mafia orientated manner (even having their own pasta making skit and Italian music playing in the background), often threatening to take Boo Boo away if he does not complete enough scares or if he is pulling pranks. The second Mr. White has blonde hair and the first Mr. White doesn't. Noticeably, the Mr. White with the hat is a higher rank than the other Mr. White, who is usually driving their family’s mode of transport. The Whites are large ghosts with dark glasses. The Mr. White with the hat and Hawaiian tie is seemingly of a higher rank than the Mr. White with blonde hair, who is usually driving their mode of transport.

characters.name: Mr. White and Mr. White
characters.description: Mr. White and Mr. Whiteare the married ghostly mentors of youngBoo Boo. Distinguished by their glasses and high rank within "The Family", the husbands typically act in a mafia orientated manner (even having their own pasta making skit and Italian music playing in the background), often threatening to take Boo Boo away if he does not complete enough scares or if he is pulling pranks. The second Mr. White has blonde hair and the first Mr. White doesn't. Noticeably, the Mr. White with the hat is a higher rank than the other Mr. White, who is usually driving their family’s mode of transport. The Whites are large ghosts with dark glasses. The Mr. White with the hat and Hawaiian tie is seemingly of a higher rank than the Mr. White with blonde hair, who is usually driving their mode of transport.

characters.name: Myopic, Malice and Misbegotten
characters.description: Myopic, Malice and MisbegottenSource The triplets are little kids that seem pretty happy for members of the misery family. They wear black clothes and have short brownish hair with wingless bees on them. They don't have veils, perhaps due to their sizes. They dance and laugh around Morose leading Morose to wail "Children, Cheerful, Playful Children... Why?! Why?!"

characters.name: Poe
characters.description: Poeis a fictional character appearing in theRuby Gloomfranchise. A sophisticated crow, he and his brothers,Edgar and Allan, are named after the famous Goth poet Edgar Allan Poe. They all live in a giant birdhouse attached toGloom Manor. "Yes, well I studied at Oxford naturally. Did I mention that I am descended from the great poet Edgar Allan Poe's pet bugie, Paco? Gloomsville is a place for me; where a gentleman can study literature at his leisure, and still find the time to give the guidance & advice that I know would be irreplaceable in my absence!" Being a crow, Poe has black feathers and a thick beak. His attire consists of a black top hat with a teal stripe, a spectacle worn over his right eye, and a purple jacket with a gold button worn over a white shirt. He is shorter and rounder thanEdgar and Allan. Poe is a talkative know-it-all. As a bird of culture and sophistication, he likes to think of himself as a role model to the rest of the mansion's residents. Unfortunately, he tends to be a little too attached to his image and likes to cover up the parts of him that make him look like less than what he makes himself out to be. For example, he'd rather tell people he's performing in an actual opera than admit that he's staying home to listen to opera records. Poe's pampered nature also tends to make him overreact to things that everyone else would more or less take in stride. He is also fond of claiming to be a descendant of Edgar Allan Poe's pet budgie Paco, which is shown in the episode "Time Flies". But despite being obsessed with his image, he is still a very caring crow and he loves his friends and his brothers very much.

characters.name: R.I.P.
characters.description: Misery's first practice session with R.I.P. R.I.P.is a rock band formed byFrank and Len.

characters.name: Ruby and Skull Boy's relationship
characters.description: Over the series, Ruby was confirmed to have a crush on Skull Boy. The only characters to know of her crush are Doom Kitty (being Ruby's pet) and Frank and Len as of the episode "Quadro-gloomia", when they were able to see and hear how Ruby felt of Skull Boy from her point of view (i.e. looking at Skull Boy dreamily as he walks to her, and her heart beating faster when Skull Boy tries to ask her a question, and when he looks into her eyes). Ruby won't admit her feelings when they confront her, asking if she wants them to find out if Skull Boy likes her in return, pretending to not know what they're talking about. She has come close to admitting her feelings. In Sunny Daze, when they're trapped in a cave, Ruby says in these situations in adventure movies, true feelings are admitted. She tries several times to tell Skull Boy, only to be interrupted by Iris. At the end of the episode, he asks her what she wanted to tell him, but she decides to save it for "another sunny day" since Iris and Misery are around. The feelings are not one-sided, as Skull Boy is implied to return her feelings. One major clue was in 'Ruby Cubed'. Skull Boy and Ruby play the title leads of Poe's play "Basil and Rosemary" respectively (a reference to Romeo and Juliet). Skull Boy has trouble saying his lines and playing a "romantic lead" when Poe tells him he needs to act like he's in love with the lead Rosemary (Ruby), which makes him blush and appear flustered, Iris and Misery not helping by playfully teasing and jeering at him when he is to perform the balcony scene (such as Misery saying "Humina Humina") with Ruby. He finds a hat that helps him recite his lines as a romantic, but it causes Iris and Misery to fall for him, becoming jealous when they think he likes Ruby, causing them to dress and act like her for his attention. In the end, Skull Boy does the scene without the hat, reciting his lines perfectly. Ruby swoons over his performance, showing he doesn't need the hat for her to fall for him (though its unknown if the hat would even affect her since she already had feelings for him). Quadro Gloomia

characters.name: Ruby Gloom (character)
characters.description: Ruby Gloomis the main title character of theanimated TV showandproduct line of the same name. She lives in a Victorian mansion with her friends in Gloomsville. She is the only one who can routinely understand what her cat,Doom Kitty, is trying to say. She serves as the voice of reason for her friends. "I love helping my friends look on the bright side of every situation! Unless of course, the dark side is their bright side, and that's okay too. It's important to be yourself and of course have a good time doing it. I love sitting withDoom Kittyand writing in my journal or dreaming up an excuse for a party with my friends or sewing or making a movie. The sky is the limit at our house - as long as its fun why not?" Ruby holding her pet, Doom Kitty. Ruby is happy-go-lucky, kindhearted, playful, and thoughtful. She is level-headed, but this doesn't make her less childlike, as she eagerly participates in silly activities with her friends. She is known as very friendly, cheerful, cute, lovely, kind, positive, helpful, loyal, voice of reason, chipper, optimistic, caring, perky, nice, polite, sometimes insane, intelligent, happy, loving, gloomy (only in some merchandise), quintessential, wholesome, considerate, welcoming, joyful, idealistic, creative, empathetic, open, nurturing, and responsible, mature, eccentric, proactive, affectionate, confident, independent, romantic, social, and supportive.

characters.name: Scaredy Bat
characters.description: Scaredy Batis a character appearing in theRuby Gloomfranchise. He is a vampire bat with an Indian accent who, as his name suggests, is afraid of almost everything. This constant state of fear is perfect forBoo Boo, the mansion's ghost, since Scaredy is the only one who is afraid of him. Even though Scaredy's fears will sometimes get the better of him, he is the loyal and incredibly talented drummer ofFrank and Len's band,R.I.P.When he is behind the kit, Scaredy is a beat machine and forgets his fears. His talent as a drummer has earned him a permanent place in Frank and Len's band. "Ooh! What was that?! Oh yes... Well actually I am sure it was nothing - just a little breeze... or an intruder... or a huge monster! Yes that's it - it is a huge life threatening monster!!! Oh - what's that? a dust bunny? It's a little dust bunny? Oh yes so it is - a little dust bunny - well no matter - one can never be too careful now can they......now where was I - ohhh is it getting darker? I am not too fond of the dark. Oh, everything makes me so nervous. It's bad enough that I am afraid of pretty much everything, but Boo Boo is constantly startling me! Thank goodness Ruby is here to help me set my nerves at ease!" Scaredy Bat is extremely fearful and anxious. Despite being a bat, he's afraid of flying and being in the dark. Scaredy is even afraid of his own reflection. He finds comfort in his friends and in holding a crumpet, his security object.
characters.personality_section: Scaredy Bat is extremely fearful and anxious. Despite being a bat, he's afraid of flying and being in the dark. Scaredy is even afraid of his own reflection. He finds comfort in his friends and in holding a crumpet, his security object.

characters.name: Skull Boy
characters.description: Skull Boyis a character appearing in theRuby Gloomfranchise. A young skeleton of many talents, Skull Boy is the mansion's official jack-of-all-trades. From filmmaking to painting, cooking to cavern exploring, robotics to private investigating, Skull Boy can pretty much do it all. His only problem is that he can't seem to find one thing and stick to it for any serious length of time. But if Skull Boy doesn't know where he's going, it's mostly because he doesn't know where he's been. Skull Boy longs to know who his family is and who his ancestors were. "I wish I knew who I was descended from! I'm a little unsure of myself, but it's hard when you're still searching for your true identity! I know I love books, science, art... Anything that makes me think. I'm pretty sure if I keep following my true passions it will lead me to what I am looking for! In the meantime I have a great group of friends who are willing to participate in my every quest." Skull Boy is a skeleton with emerald green eyes. He normally dresses in a black turtleneck shirt, a black belt with a skull buckle and a chain, gray pants, and red and white sneakers. Skull Boy is a curious, smart, imaginative skeleton who craves belonging.
characters.personality_section: Skull Boy is a curious, smart, imaginative skeleton who craves belonging.

characters.name: Squig
characters.description: Squigis a large flying worm thatIrisadopted as a pet. Although he is not seen that often, he accompanies Iris on all her adventures as both her companion and sometimes her mode of transport. He is about five times as big as Iris, is black and red with patterns down his back, and has a small set of wings at the end of his tail. Iris was on one of her adventures on a boat in the ocean, and decided to take a break. She threw her anchor to the bottom of the ocean, and wasn't aware that Squig was on it because he was really small. The anchor landed on top of an octopus' head, and got upset at Squig. Squig was frightened and swam up to the top of the ocean as fast as he could, and grabbed a rope that was attached to Iris' boat. He then started swimming and moving the boat, making Iris happy as she was enjoying the ride. They've been friends since then.

characters.name: The Bunny
characters.description: The Bunnyis the main antagonist of theRuby Gloomepisode "Bad Hare Day". The bunny pretends to be cute and loving with a cold, but is actually evil. A presumed kleptomaniac, the bunny wants to steal everything fromGloom Manor, althoughDoom Kittyis the only one who knows this at first. Later, everyone else finds out that the bunny is evil when Doom Kitty cages him and they see their stuff in his carriage, so they kick him out. The bunny appears as a pink rabbit with red eyes. Although he is referred to as a bunny, it is possible he's actually an adult pretending to be young. The bunny pretends to be cute and loving with a cold, but is actually evil. A presumed kleptomaniac, the bunny wants to steal everything fromGloom Manor, althoughDoom Kittyis the only one who knows this at first. Later, everyone else finds out that the bunny is evil when Doom Kitty cages him and they see their stuff in his carriage, so they kick him out.

characters.name: The Moon
characters.description: The Moonis a recurring character onRuby Gloom. She always shows up in the sky at night, and watches Ruby and the gang's many adventures and activities from up above. SHE has two sides, one which is a light gray, and the other side a dark blue, known as "the dark side"; both of the sides each have one face. While she mostly doesn't talk, her voice varies throughout the series, whether it be in a deep voice whenever she yawns, or in a high-pitched voice when she laughs in the episode "Broken Records". She plays a significant role in the episode "Out of This World", in which the mansion - along with Ruby, Poe, Skull Boy, Iris, Misery, Boo-Boo, Doom Kitty, and Scaredy Bat - ends up floating up to her when it goes to space. She was so scared, she chattered her teeth, and the house ended up getting stuck in her mouth, until she sneezed it out. Soon after, the house starts orbiting around her for a long time, causing her to get annoyed, then tired, then nauseous as it goes on.

characters.name: The Skele-Tunes
characters.description: The Skele-Tunesare a group of jazz musicians who befriendSkull Boyin the episode "Skull Boys Don't Cry", nicknaming him "SB". They also appear in "Hair(less): The Musical", and make cameos in other episodes. The lead vocalist, Skele-T, is notably the only member who actually wears clothes, sporting a gold tooth, matching earrings, a white bowler hat, and a checkered shirt. The other skeletons, whom Skele-T refers to as "The Bones" each have a different instrument; one with an accordion, one with a tuba and one using his rib cage as a xylophone. They also differ in size, shape and bone tone. Skele-T does most of the talking, using confusing slang phrases such as, "You're straight from the fridge, daddio" and throwing in skeleton puns like "You fracture me" instead of "You crack me up". The Skele-Tunes find messing with people hilarious. They hid every time Skull Boy tried to introduce them to his friends (until the end of the episode). Messing with his head really "fractured" them. When Skull Boy leavesGloom Manorto try and find his roots in "Hair(Less): The Musical", Skele-T even invites him to join their band, but Skull Boy eventually understands where he needs to be regardless of knowing where he came from.
